title: nextID ignores closed/ subdir -> reallocates an in-use ID (collision)
description:
## Bug
`Store.nextID` (store.go:219-240) computes the high-water mark from `os.ReadDir(s.dir)` of the hot dir only. Once closed issues move to `.tasks/closed/`, nextID stops seeing them and will re-allocate an ID already used by a closed issue -> overwrite/collision.

## Evidence (benchmark, dtctl-test corpus)
With 378 closed moved to closed/ and 41 open in the hot dir, `Create` allocated an ID derived from only the 41 open files, ignoring every closed ID. In a numeric agt-#### scheme this is a guaranteed collision generator.

## Fix
nextID must take its high-water mark across ALL partitions (hot + closed/ + any cold/parked dir). A dir listing is enough — no parse. Related to the broader ID strategy in at-2fb.

## Acceptance
- [ ] After closing the highest-numbered issue, the next Create allocates a strictly greater ID.
- [ ] Regression test with a populated closed/ dir.
- [ ] Must land with / before the closed/ move (at-zib.2).

title: bwb: implement Repository over the agent-tasks SDK
description:
## Description
Make beads-workbench read/write a .tasks store directly via github.com/hk9890/agent-tasks/sdk/tasks, removing the bd subprocess path.

## Instructions
- Add the sdk module to bwb go.mod (require + local replace during dev).
- Implement the existing Repository interface against tasks.Store (Dashboard, Issue, Search, Create/Update/Close, AddComment, Catalogs, HealthCheck).
- Map tasks.Issue/Detail/Ref onto bwb domain types.
- Delete internal/bd and internal/repository/beads (subprocess, argv contract, JSON decode, bd workarounds) once the new impl is wired.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bwb builds and runs against a .tasks store with no bd process spawned.
- [ ] Dashboard, detail, search, and all mutations work against .tasks.
- [ ] internal/bd and the beads repo impl are removed.

title: agent-tasks v1 — replace beads for bwb and agents
description:
## Goal
Make agent-tasks the task backend for both consumers: beads-workbench (via the Go SDK) and the agent workflow (via the atctl CLI), retiring bd for these uses.

## Success criteria
- bwb reads/writes a .tasks store with no bd subprocess.
- The agent workflow drives atctl instead of bd.
- CI builds and tests both modules.
- Open design decisions (IDs, license) are resolved.

See SPEC.md for the design contract.

title: Split All() (hot) from Archive()/AllIncludingClosed() for export + viewer
description:
## Scope
Today `All()` means 'everything in the flat dir'. After the split it means 'hot/open'. Callers that need the full history must opt in explicitly:
- ready/list/Labels(default) -> hot only (cheap).
- export, full-text search, bwb viewer history, all-time Labels -> an explicit `Archive()`/`AllIncludingClosed()` that also reads closed/ (and parked/).
- Document the semantics so no caller silently loses closed issues.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Hot-path APIs are O(open).
- [ ] An explicit accessor returns hot+closed for export/viewer.
- [ ] bwb can still render closed issues.

title: Ref resolution + checkRefs fall through to closed/
description:
## Scope
Open issues routinely reference closed ones (open child of a closed epic; blocked_by a closed issue). With closed moved out of the hot index:
- `Get(id)` and Detail parent/blocker resolution must fall through to `.tasks/closed/`.
- `checkRefs` must validate refs that live in closed/ via a cheap `stat` (no parse), not report them missing.
- ready-computation may treat 'blocker absent from hot set' as satisfied, but stat closed/ to distinguish a genuinely-closed blocker from a dangling/typo'd ID (else broken refs silently read as unblocked).

## Acceptance
- [ ] Creating/updating an open issue whose parent/blocker is closed validates OK.
- [ ] A dangling ref (neither hot nor closed) still errors.
- [ ] ready() correct when blockers are closed.

title: Close moves file to closed/ and freezes it; add Reopen (move back)
description:
## Scope
- `Close` atomically moves the issue .md from the hot dir into `.tasks/closed/` and marks it immutable (writes to a closed record are rejected at the store layer).
- `Reopen` moves it back to the hot dir and re-enables writes.
- The comment sidecar stays append-only even for closed issues (matches the observed dtctl pattern of post-close verification notes) — freeze the record, not the audit log.
- Git rename keeps history clean.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Close relocates the file; a subsequent Update/Close-edit on a closed record errors (except Reopen).
- [ ] Reopen restores mutability.
- [ ] Appending a comment to a closed issue still works.

title: Sidecar format + read/write: append-only markdown comments
description:
Implement the comment sidecar as the storage primitive.

## Scope
- One sidecar per issue, comments stored verbatim as markdown (no YAML/JSON escaping).
- Append is O_APPEND (no whole-file rewrite, no re-encode of prior comments).
- Per-comment header carries author + created timestamp on its own line, e.g. `<!-- @author 2026-05-26T18:00:03Z -->` then the verbatim body, blank-line separated.
- Location must be invisible to `store.All()`. `All()` skips subdirectories and non-.md, so a `comments/<id>.md` subdir works with no All() change (confirmed in benchmark).
- Reader parses records back to []Comment; choose a start-of-line sentinel and escape/encode the rare body that contains it (SDK is the sole writer).

## Acceptance
- [ ] Append + read round-trips arbitrary bodies byte-exact (code fences, trailing whitespace, CRLF).
- [ ] No reachable input forces single-line escaped output.
- [ ] `store.All()` never opens a sidecar.

title: Rewire AddComment/Get/Detail to sidecars; drop comments from frontmatter + migrate
description:
Wire the sidecar into the store API and remove comments from the YAML frontmatter.

## Scope
- `AddComment` appends to the sidecar instead of rewriting the issue file. Decide whether a comment bumps the issue `updated` (cheap write) or is derived from sidecar mtime.
- `Get`/`Detail` load the sidecar lazily (single-issue view only); `All()`/ready/list never read it.
- Remove `Comments` from the frontmatter struct (frontmatter.go) and `Issue` marshal path.
- One-time migration: move existing inline frontmatter comments into sidecars.

## Evidence
Comments are ~1/3 of issue-file bytes; removing them cut All() 14.9ms->7.3ms and hot bytes 2.03MB->1.35MB at N=419 (dtctl-test corpus).

## Acceptance
- [ ] Frontmatter no longer contains comments; existing stores migrate cleanly.
- [ ] AddComment does not rewrite the issue .md.
- [ ] All()/ready cost drops measurably (benchmark in bench/).

title: [epic] Cold-partition closed issues into a closed/ subdir (immutable)
description:
## Motivation
Closed issues are 90% of a mature store (dtctl-test: 378 closed / 419 total) yet sit in the same flat dir that `store.All()` parses inside the write lock on every mutation. This is the dominant O(N) scaling cost.

## Measured payoff (dtctl-test corpus, 419 issues)
- Today (flat dir, comments inline):           hot=419 files, 2.03MB, All() scan **14.9ms**
- Comments -> sidecar:                          hot=419 files, 1.35MB, All() scan **7.3ms**
- Sidecar + closed/ subdir (hot = 41 open):     hot=41 files, 148KB, All() scan **0.56ms**  (**27x** faster, and flat as closed grows)

`All()` already skips subdirectories (store.go:184), so a closed/ subdir is excluded from the hot scan with no change to All() — confirmed: returned 41, zero closed leaked.

## Decision
On close, move the issue file to `.tasks/closed/` and freeze the record (immutable). Reopen = move back. The comment sidecar stays append-only even for closed issues (matches the observed dtctl pattern of post-close verification notes). Consider routing long-parked/deferred issues to the cold partition too (active-vs-inactive, not strictly open-vs-closed).

title: [epic] Move comments out of frontmatter into append-only sidecars
description:
## Motivation
Comments live in the YAML frontmatter (`Comments []Comment yaml:"comments"`). Two measured problems against the dtctl-test corpus (419 real issues, 646 comments):

1. **YAML mangles real bodies.** yaml.v3 abandons the readable block scalar (`|`) and emits a one-line double-quoted string with literal \n whenever a body has trailing whitespace, a whitespace-only line, or CRLF — exactly what pasted terminal output / verification logs contain. Measured: 8/419 files already degraded this way; data roundtrips byte-exact but the on-disk artifact is unreadable and git-diff-hostile.
2. **Comments bloat the hot scan.** Comments are ~1/3 of issue-file bytes. `store.All()` (run inside the write lock on every Create/Update/AddDep) parses them on every call. Measured: moving comments to sidecars cut All() from 14.9ms -> 7.3ms at N=419 and shrank hot bytes 2.03MB -> 1.35MB.

## Decision
Store comments in an **append-only markdown sidecar**, body verbatim (no escaping, ever). Chosen over JSONL (makes every body a \n-escaped blob) and YAML sidecar (same escaping bug relocated). Format probed in /tmp experiment; markdown is the only option that fixes readability AND scan cost.

title: decision: ID collision strategy
description:
## Description
IDs are sequential prefix-NNNN by scan (no counter file), so parallel branches can collide on merge. Decide the strategy.

## Options
- Keep sequential + add atctl doctor to detect/renumber collisions.
- Switch to collision-resistant IDs (short random suffix).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded in SPEC.md; chosen approach implemented.

title: CI: build and test both modules
description:
## Description
GitHub Actions workflow for the repo.

## Instructions
- Build atctl; run make fmt-check vet test (root + sdk modules).
- Cache Go modules.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI runs on push/PR and fails on fmt/vet/test errors in either module.

title: agents: drive atctl instead of bd
description:
## Description
Make the agent task workflow use atctl against a .tasks store instead of bd.

## Open question
Adapt the existing beads-tasks plugin skills, or create a new atctl-tasks plugin? Decide first.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An agent can discover work (ready), claim, comment, and close via atctl.
- [ ] Skill/agent docs reference atctl commands, not bd.

title: [decision] active-vs-inactive partition: route deferred/long-parked to cold too
description:
## Question
The hot/cold split is sound, but the real partition may be active-vs-inactive, not strictly open-vs-closed. Long-parked/aspirational/deferred issues inflate the hot set without being actionable (dtctl-test already had 6 deferred).

## Options
- closed/ only (simplest; backlog stays hot).
- closed/ + parked/ (route deferred + long-untouched here; keeps hot = truly active).

## Notes
Hot scan is O(open) at ~13us/file, so even ~2000 hot is ~27ms — degrades gracefully; this is an optimization, not a correctness issue. Decide before compaction/compression design.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Decision recorded in SPEC.md.

title: atctl: agent guide (howto/commands catalog)
description:
## Description
A bd-prime equivalent for atctl: a machine-readable command catalog and/or an LLM-oriented howto, like dtctl commands/howto, so agents can self-serve the surface.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] atctl exposes a concise agent-facing usage guide (command list + examples).

title: decision: choose a license
description:
## Description
No LICENSE committed yet (private repo). dtctl uses Apache-2.0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] License chosen and LICENSE file added (or explicitly deferred).
